data.body: Closed Gerrit workflow readiness gaps in the main checkout: (1) added 'gerrit' remote (https:///a/rebar, no embedded username — keychain resolves identity; fetch verified, gerrit/main == origin/main); (2) installed the Gerrit 3.14.1 commit-msg hook (.git/hooks/commit-msg, Change-Id stamping verified present); (3) refreshed stale [host]:29418 known_hosts entry via keyscan (old file backed up to known_hosts.old) — SSH now cleanly reports publickey-denied, i.e. transport fixed, no SSH key registered on a Gerrit account yet (optional; HTTP covers the full flow). Remaining human steps: provision a contributor Gerrit account via GitHub OAuth + HTTP password (stop riding the rebar-review-bot identity for pushes); bot HTTP password rotation should be COORDINATED (deployed review-bot + SSM hold it — do not regenerate unilaterally). Deliberately did NOT push a synthetic test change (would trigger billable LLM review + CI on the live gate); S5 E2E covers write-path proof.
